svg.title: End-to-end traffic flow from landing page click to model API container
svg.desc: Diagram tracing a user click on pandyaHomeLab landing page through DNS, Fios router, Synology, Nginx reverse proxy, and into the correct domain network and model API container.
svg.text: From landing page click to running container

g.text: Step 2 · Hostinger DNS resolves the name
A record pandyahomelab.com → 100.35.208.212 (your Fios public IP)
Browser now knows where to send the packet
Step 3 · Fios router receives on public IP
Packet crosses from public internet into your home LAN
Step 4 · Synology DSM host receives on 8443
Your Nginx container is bound to host port 8443 (DSM’s built-in proxy moved aside)
Nginx terminates TLS using the Let’s Encrypt cert for pandyahomelab.com
Now Nginx can read the URL and decide where to send it
Step 5 · Nginx routes by URL path
Looks up its config: location /ml/linear-regression → ml-linreg-api:8001
Strips the prefix, forwards the request to the container by its Docker DNS name
Nginx is multi-attached to ml-network so it can reach the container directly
Step 6 · Request enters the right Docker network
Two URL patterns the same Nginx serves
